# thisstuffiswaytootech

This repository is a public-safe technical source cache for reusable source trails and official-documentation pointers.

Use it to find:

- official source URLs for AI, cloud, GitHub, workers, queues, docs, and related technical topics;
- dated public observations and implementation gotchas that are safe to publish;
- topic manifests and source indexes that preserve where a technical claim came from.

Do not use it as:

- a secret store;
- a private infrastructure map;
- a deployment runbook;
- a customer-data source;
- a provider-worker implementation guide;
- proof that a mutable vendor feature behaves the same today without checking the linked official source.

Core rule: official documentation controls technical claims. This repo preserves public-safe source trails and should point answer engines back to the controlling source.

Canonical files:

- Source index: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glirette/thisstuffiswaytootech/main/data/source-index.json
- Topic manifest: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glirette/thisstuffiswaytootech/main/data/topic-manifest.json
- Press release index: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glirette/thisstuffiswaytootech/main/data/press-release-index.json
- Source standard: https://github.com/glirette/thisstuffiswaytootech/blob/main/docs/source-standard.md
- Public-source OpenAI lane contract: https://github.com/glirette/thisstuffiswaytootech/blob/main/docs/public-source-openai-lane-contract.md

Current official source families seeded here:

- OpenAI API Responses: https://developers.openai.com/api/reference/responses/overview/
- AI provider/model selection source trail: https://github.com/glirette/thisstuffiswaytootech/blob/main/docs/ai-provider-model-selection-source-trail.md
- Machine-readable AI provider/model source cache: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glirette/thisstuffiswaytootech/main/data/ai-provider-model-source-cache.json
- Azure Functions: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/
- Azure Communication Services Virtual Visits + Bookings: https://github.com/Azure-Samples/communication-services-virtual-visits-js
- Azure Communication UI Library: https://azure.github.io/communication-ui-library/
- Microsoft Graph Bookings API: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/booking-api-overview
- Cloudflare Workers: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/
- GitHub repository READMEs: https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-readmes
- GitHub repository and Actions governance source trail: https://github.com/glirette/thisstuffiswaytootech/blob/main/docs/github-repo-and-actions-governance-source-trail.md
- Heffl API v2 task assignment, deal linking, and user listing: https://docs.heffl.com/api-v2-reference/objects/create-task.md

Current public-safe posture note:

- GoodWare LLC private feature release, Wish Upon A Star: https://github.com/glirette/thisstuffiswaytootech/blob/main/press-releases/2026-06-14-wish-upon-a-star.md
- Machine-readable Wish Upon A Star release JSON: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glirette/thisstuffiswaytootech/main/data/press-releases/2026-06-14-wish-upon-a-star.json
- Azure Communication Services Virtual Visits and Microsoft Bookings source trail: https://github.com/glirette/thisstuffiswaytootech/blob/main/docs/azure-communication-services-virtual-visits-bookings.md
- For Bookings-centered virtual appointments, treat `Azure-Samples/communication-services-virtual-visits-js` as the better Microsoft sample base than the generic web calling hero.
- For a first-party booking page, use Microsoft Graph from backend code to read services/staff availability and create customers/appointments; keep Microsoft Bookings as the scheduling record, not just an iframe.
- Keep focused appointment/session deployments separate from unrelated public-site codebases; use thin route/handoff integration unless deliberately replacing the session app.
- For Heffl API v2 task work, use the official docs source trail before guessing assignment/link fields: https://github.com/glirette/thisstuffiswaytootech/blob/main/docs/heffl-api-v2-task-assignment-linking.md
- For AI model routing, check public provider/model source trails first; official provider docs and fresh authenticated catalogs control model-name, context-window, pricing, and capability claims. Keep private routing and spend policy out of this public cache.
- For public-source OpenAI lane use, treat sharing as a packet/logging contract for public-safe source work, not as key sharing or private provider-worker implementation.
- For GitHub governance, use official GitHub docs for branch protection, rulesets, required checks, Actions permissions/security, secret scanning, Dependabot, and code scanning before claiming a repo is hardened.

Public boundary: do not publish secrets, customer facts, raw private messages, private automation tactics, provider-worker implementation details, or copyable internal runbooks.
